shops.name: Pellem's Fur Store
shops.description: The Pellem's Fur Store is a fur shop run by Pellem in the south-eastern corner of the Hunter Guild.

stock.Furs required: Polar kebbit fur x2
Common kebbit fur x2
Feldip weasel fur x2
Desert devil fur x2
Larupia fur or Tatty larupia fur
Larupia fur or Tatty larupia fur
Graahk fur or Tatty graahk fur
Graahk fur or Tatty graahk fur
Kyatt fur or Tatty kyatt fur
Kyatt fur or Tatty kyatt fur

shops.name: Chouani
shops.description: Chouani is a tanner in Auburnvale, located on the west side of the town. There is a loom in her shop. Chouani is as expensive as Sbott, being more expensive than the standard tanners.

shops.name: Tanner
shops.description: A tanner is a non-player character who, for a price, will tan unnoted hides into leather:

stock.Name: Ellis
stock.Notes: Both the ring of dueling and the amulet of glory can be used to teleport to Al Kharid, and Ellis is near a bank.

stock.Name: Leatherworker
stock.Notes: Requires 40 Ranged, and does not have a right-click option to open the shop interface.

stock.Name: Sbott
stock.Notes: He charges higher prices for tanning, a bit over twice what other tanners charge.

stock.Name: Eodan
stock.Notes: He tans hides at a higher cost than other tanners, but this can be reduced by completing parts of the Kourend & Kebos Diary.

stock.Name: Mary
stock.Notes: Requires the completion of Getting Ahead to use.

stock.Name: Chouani
stock.Notes: Requires the completion of Children of the Sun to use. Her tanning prices are the same as Sbott's.

shops.name: Sawmill
shops.description: A sawmill is a shop which also serves as a facility, and facilitates the creation of planks. The sawmill operator working there can turn seven different kinds of logs into planks for a fee, and also sells Construction supplies: saws, bolts of cloth, and three kinds of nails; they share the same stoc

shops.name: Portal (Player-owned house)
shops.description: The house portal, commonly referred to as a POH portal, is the entrance to a player-owned house (POH), the main feature of the Construction skill. House Advertisements are also found next to these portals, in which players can check whether advertised houses contain frequently used features.

shops.name: Construction
shops.description: Construction is a members-only skill that allows players to build their own houses and provide furniture for them. To begin Construction, players must buy a house from one of the estate agents. A starter house will cost 1,000 coins, and will be in Rimmington. A level 40 Magic spell can then be used

stock.Cost per Exp: There are multiple spawn locations for planks, see the item page for more information. Planks are made from regular logs by taking them to a sawmill operator. Planks can be bought in the Razmire Builders Merchants shop, located in Mort'ton. Nails can be bought at the Construction Supplies shop, run by the sawmill operator north-east of Varrock or in the Woodcutting Guild, Nails can be created using the Smithing skill.
Oak planks are made from oak logs by taking them to a sawmill operator. Oak planks are dropped by several monsters, see the item page for more information.
Teak planks are made from teak logs by taking them to a sawmill operator. Teak planks are dropped by several monsters, see the item page for more information.
Mahogany planks are made from Mahogany logs by taking them to a sawmill operator. Mahogany planks are dropped by several monsters, see the item page for more information.

Soft clay can be created by using a container of water (e.g. a jug of water or bucket of water) on clay, by casting the Humidify spell while having clay in your inventory, or by mining the clay rock whilst wearing a bracelet of clay. Soft clay packs can be bought from Prospector Percy's Nugget Shop in Motherlode Mine for 10 golden nuggets each, contain 100 soft clay per pack. Soft clay is dropped by several monsters, see the item page for more information.
Bolts of cloth can be bought at the Construction Supplies shop, run by the sawmill operator north-east of Varrock or in the Woodcutting Guild,

Steel bars can be smelted using the Smithing skill at level 30 Smithing by using one iron ore and two coal on a furnace. Steel bars are dropped by several monsters, see the item page for more information.

Limestone bricks can be crafted through the Crafting skill at level 12 by using a chisel with limestone, Limestone bricks can be bought in the Razmire Builders Merchants shop, located in Mort'ton. Limestone bricks can be bought in the Keldagrim Stonemason shop, located in Keldagrim. Limestone bricks are dropped by several monsters, see the item page for more information.
Gold leaves can be bought in the Keldagrim Stonemason shop, located in Keldagrim. Gold leaves can be bought in the Stonecutter Supplies shop, located in the Stonecutter Outpost.
Marble blocks can be bought in the Keldagrim Stonemason shop, located in Keldagrim. Marble blocks can be bought in the Stonecutter Supplies shop, located in the Stonecutter Outpost.
Magic stones can be bought in the Keldagrim Stonemason shop, located in Keldagrim. Magic stones can be bought in the Stonecutter Supplies shop, located in the Stonecutter Outpost.
Condensed gold can be bought in the Keldagrim Stonemason shop, located in Keldagrim. Condensed gold can be bought in the Stonecutter Supplies shop, located in the Stonecutter Outpost.

stock.Levelrequirement: Parlour(Chair space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 2 Planks 2 Nails

stock.Levelrequirement: Parlour(Bookcase space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 4 Planks 4 Nails

stock.Levelrequirement: Parlour(Chair space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 2 Oak planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Dining room(Table space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 4 Oak planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Kitchen(Larder space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 8 Oak planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Quest Hall(Guild trophy space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 1 Mythical cape (given back when removed) 3 Teak planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Dining room(Table space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 6 Mahogany planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Superior garden(Seating space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 6 Teak planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Dungeon(Door space)
stock.Cost per Exp: 10 Oak planks

stock.Levelrequirement: Costume room
stock.Uses: Treasure chest Armour case Magic wardrobe Cape rack Toy box Fancy dress box
stock.Cost: The most notable function of this room is the possibility to store many different items. E.g. minigame rewards, Treasure Trails rewards, armour sets and Holiday rewards.

stock.Levelrequirement: Chapel
stock.Cost: Player-owned house altars provide a place to sacrifice bones for greater Prayer experience than burying them, with the gilded altar giving up to 350% Prayer experience with both incense burners lit. The functionality of incense burners is to boost the Prayer experience bonus.

stock.Levelrequirement: Portal chamber
stock.Uses: Portals (to teleport to various locations around Gielinor) Centrepiece
stock.Cost: Within a Portal Chamber, players can use their Magic skill to build portals that they and their guests can use to teleport to various locations around Gielinor. The centrepiece is required for the house owner to direct the portals.

stock.Levelrequirement: Superior garden
stock.Cost: In this room players can plant a fairy ring (code DIQ) or spirit tree, allowing quick transportation, provided they have completed the quests required to use them. Pools will restore certain stats, depending on the type of pool built.

stock.Levelrequirement: Portal nexus
stock.Uses: The Portal nexus Mounted xeric's talisman Mounted digsite pendant
stock.Cost: The portal nexus can be directed to teleport the player to multiple locations. The main advantage over the Portal Chamber is the ability to have all teleports combined into one portal. Providing unlimited Xeric's talisman charges. Providing unlimited Digsite pendant charges.

stock.Levelrequirement: Achievement gallery
stock.Uses: Altar Adventure log Jewellery box Boss lair display
stock.Cost: The altar allows players to switch spellbooks without having to travel to the overworld spellbook-switching altars. The Adventure log allows players to show off some of their achievements. The Jewellery box can store various teleportation jewellery. Each box will provide unlimited teleports to locations provided by the enchanted jewellery. The Boss lair display allows a player to build a fancy display showing off various bosses. In order to display a boss lair, players must have killed the boss at least once.

stock.Levelrequirement: Quest Hall(Guild trophy space)
Portal Chamber(Centrepiece space)
stock.Cost (GE): 2 Limestone bricks for the teleport focus 3 Teak planks for the teak portal 500-1,500 runes of a unique combination to connect the portal to the destination

stock.Levelrequirement: Portal nexus(Amulet space)
stock.Cost (GE): 1 Mahogany plank 1 Gold leaf 1 Xeric's talisman (inert) 5,000 Lizardman fangs

stock.Levelrequirement: Superior garden(Teleport space)
Superior garden(Teleport space)

stock.Levelrequirement: Portal nexus(Amulet space)
stock.Cost (GE): 1 Mahogany plank 1 Gold leaf 1 Curator's medallion

stock.Levelrequirement: Superior garden(Teleport space)
Achievement Gallery(Jewellery box space)
stock.Cost (GE): 1 Gold leaf 5 Skills necklace(4)s 5 Combat bracelet(4)s

stock.Levelrequirement: Achievement Gallery(Jewellery box space)
stock.Cost (GE): 2 Gold leaves 8 Amulet of glory(4)s 8 Ring of wealth (5)s

stock.Levelrequirement: Superior garden(Teleport space)
Superior garden(Pool space)
stock.Restores: 5 Limestone bricks 5 Buckets of water 1,000 Soul runes 1,000 Body runes

Superior garden(Pool space)

stock.GE price: Special attack energy Run energy Prayer points Restores reduced stats

stock.Levelrequirement: Superior garden(Pool space)

shops.name: Knife
shops.description: A knife is a multi-functional tool used for a large variety of tasks, primarily used for fletching logs in the Fletching skill and cutting food in the Cooking skill. It sees secondary usage in the Crafting skill. Additionally, it can be used to cut through webs and setup Hunter traps.

shops.name: Zaff's Superior Staffs!
shops.description: Zaff's Superior Staffs is a staff shop located near central Varrock, owned by Zaff. It is the main source of battlestaves and of elemental staves for free players.

shops.name: Mithril axe
shops.description: A mithril axe is an axe stronger than the black axe, and weaker than the adamant axe. Like all mithril weapons, it requires level 20 Attack to wield. It can be used in combat or in Woodcutting to cut trees. To cut a tree with this axe, a player must have 21 Woodcutting.

shops.name: Adamant axe
shops.description: An adamant axe is a one-handed axe requiring 30 Attack to wield and 31 Woodcutting to use to cut trees. Making one requires 1 adamant bar and 71 Smithing, yielding 62.5 Smithing experience. As with all axes, it can be used to cut trees even when in the player's inventory.

shops.name: Woodcutting
shops.description: Woodcutting is a gathering skill that primarily involves chopping trees to obtain logs used in the Fletching, Firemaking, and Construction skills. Players with a higher Woodcutting level can chop down new types of trees and cut down trees quicker, especially as higher levels of axes become available

stock.Members: Can be used to cut most trees.
The only axe capable of cutting Undead trees, however unable to chop any other type. Used in the Animal Magnetism quest. Undead trees are NPCs and have a 18 Slayer level requirement to cut.
Can be used to cut most trees. The best axes available in Free-to-Play.
Special attack: Lumber Up, provides a visible +3 boost to Woodcutting.

Provides the same benefits as a dragon axe, including the special attack, however burns 1/3 of logs cut. This grants 1/2 the Firemaking experience that would normally be gained from doing so. Grants no additional benefit to trees that provide resources that cannot be burned. When depleted of charges, it behaves as a dragon axe.

Special attack: Lumber Up, provides a visible +3 boost to Woodcutting. Requires crystal shards to operate; as such, chopping very low level trees (such as oak and below) with the crystal axe is not recommended as players with 71 Woodcutting likely have maxed out the chance to receive a log. When depleted of charges, it behaves as a dragon axe. Requires completion of Song of the Elves to use.
Can be used to cut most trees.
Special attack: Lumber Up, provides a visible +3 boost to Woodcutting.

Special attack: Lumber Up, provides a visible +3 boost to Woodcutting. Requires crystal shards to operate; as such, chopping very low level trees (such as oak and below) with the crystal felling axe is not recommended as players with 71 Woodcutting likely have maxed out the chance to receive a log. When depleted of charges, it behaves as a dragon felling axe. Requires completion of Song of the Elves to use.

stock.Notes: Can be used to cut Light jungle, Medium jungle and, Dense jungle.
Chance to double logs from normal trees.
Requires completion of the medium Kandarin Diary.
Requires completion of the hard Kandarin Diary.
Requires completion of the elite Kandarin Diary.
Gives 20% increased chance to receive a specific type of bird nest dropped from trees whilst Woodcutting.
Slightly increases bird's eggs and rings when receiving a bird nest.
Doubles the chance to receive easy-elite clue nests when in the wilderness.
Provides a 10% chance to not use a charge when using a crystal axe.
Requiring level 99 in the Woodcutting skill, grants a 10% increase in chance to receive a bird nest. Temporary +1 visible boost to Woodcutting.
Same effect as the Woodcutting cape, except for the visible boost.
Stores a variety of Forestry related items.
Can carry an additional 28 logs of any type, increasing the number of logs a player can carry to a total of 56.
Doubles the amount of leaves obtained from Forestry events.
Offers a 60-80% chance of receiving an additional log.
Used during and after the Animal magnetism quest to chop twigs from undead trees.

shops.name: Battleaxe
shops.description: Battleaxes are one of several powerful melee weapons that can deal large amounts of damage. Although they can hit hard, battleaxes have low attack speed. They are stronger than longswords, but have slower attack speeds. Battleaxes are also weaker than two-handed swords, but slightly faster. Players

shops.name: Adamant battleaxe
shops.description: The adamant battleaxe is a battleaxe that is stronger than a mithril battleaxe, but weaker than a rune battleaxe. Like most adamant weapons, 30 Attack is required in order to wield it.

shops.name: Farming
shops.description: Farming is a skill in which players plant seeds and harvest crops. The crops grown range from fruit, vegetables, herbs, and hops, to wood-bearing trees and cacti, to even mushrooms and coral. The harvested items have a wide variety of uses, and are popular for training Herblore and Cooking. Many pla

shops.name: Agility
shops.description: Agility is a members-only skill which gives access to various shortcuts around Gielinor. Other benefits of the skill include faster run energy restoration, slower run energy dissipation while running, and the ability to wear weight-saving outfits like the graceful outfit, as well as wield certain to

stock.Name: Rocks
stock.Notes: Upper rocks leading from desert to Agility Pyramid.

stock.Name: Bridge
stock.Notes: Must have started Nature Spirit to access Mort Myre Swamp.

stock.Name: Ropeswing
stock.Notes: Requires a rope to make the swing and will not be returned on use.

stock.Name: Trellis
stock.Notes: Used as the starting point for the Watchtower quest.

stock.Name: Gap
stock.Notes: Must have partial completion of Watchtower to gain access to Gu'Tanoth. The jump requires a cost of 20 to pay the guard each jump.

stock.Name: Crevice
stock.Notes: Must have started Royal Trouble to access the dungeon. Requires no level to go backwards through the crevice.

stock.Name: Tunnel
stock.Notes: Must have started Royal Trouble to access the dungeon. Requires no level to go backwards through the tunnel.

shops.name: Vale Totems (minigame)
shops.description: Vale Totems is a Fletching minigame found in Auburn Valley in the north western part of Varlamore. Players earn vale offerings and vale research points by building, carving, and decorating totems around the valley.

shops.name: Sawmill Operator (Auburnvale)
shops.description: Sawmill Operator can be found in Auburnvale north of the Auburn Pub. He can turn seven kinds of logs into planks (see sawmill), and also runs a Construction supplies shop selling bolts of cloth, three kinds of nails, and saws.

shops.name: Sbott
shops.description: Sbott is the werewolf tanner on the northern side of Canifis. His shop is marked with the tanning icon (). His tannery is more expensive than average, charging a bit more than double what most other tanners charge to tan a hide. However his shop is very close to the CKS fairy ring in Canifis as well

shops.name: Canifis
shops.description: Canifis, formerly known as Kharyrll, is a small town in Morytania inhabited by werewolves in human form. The town is only accessible to players who have completed the Priest in Peril quest. Canifis is the starting point for the Myreque storyline.

stock.Name: Stillness
stock.Unlock details: Unlocked in the Myreque Hideout south of Canifis

shops.name: Grand Exchange
shops.description: The Grand Exchange, commonly referred to as the GE, is a trading system for players to purchase and sell tradeable items in Old School RuneScape. It was released on 26 February 2015 following a content poll where it passed by 76.3%, narrowly passing the 75% threshold. Members get eight Grand Exchang

stock.Item Sink List: Chambers of Xeric uniques Ancestral hat Ancestral robe top Ancestral robe bottom Dinh's bulwark Elder maul Kodai wand Prayer scrolls (Arcane, Dexterous) Twisted buckler Twisted bow Theatre of Blood uniques Avernic defender hilt Sanguinesti staff Scythe of vitur Tombs of Amascut uniques Elidinis' ward Lightbearer Masori mask (f) Masori body (f) Masori chaps (f) Osmumten's fang Tumeken's shadow Dagannoth Kings uniques Archers ring Berserker ring God Wars Dungeon uniques Armadyl armour Bandos armour (except Bandos boots) Godswords (Ancient, Armadyl, Bandos, Saradomin, Zamorak) Torva full helm Torva platebody Torva platelegs Zamorakian spear Zaryte crossbow Zaryte vambraces Nightmare uniques Inquisitor's armour Inquisitor's mace Nightmare staff and eldritch, harmonised, and volatile orbs Slayer uniques Abyssal bludgeon Dark bow Dragon hunter lance Smoke battlestaff Trident of the seas (uncharged only) Zenyte shard Uncut zenyte and cut Zenyte Amulet of torture Necklace of anguish Ring of suffering Tormented bracelet Arcane and spectral spirit shields Burning claws Dragon pickaxe Dragon warhammer Toxic blowpipe

shops.name: Butterfly net
shops.description: A butterfly net is an item used in the Hunter skill. It is wielded to capture implings, and butterflies or moths throughout Gielinor and Puro-Puro, and bats in the Chambers of Xeric. Catching an impling without an impling jar will automatically loot it, and catching a butterfly or moth without a but

shops.name: Coins
shops.description: Coins (also referred to as gold pieces, gp, gold, or simply money) are the most common form of currency in Gielinor. They are used among players and non-player characters alike to exchange for items and services.

shops.name: Butterfly jar
shops.description: A butterfly jar is used to contain captured butterflies or moths with the Hunter skill. These jars can also be turned into impling jars by treating them with imp repellent. Butterfly jars cannot be used to hold implings. They can be created by using one charge of a jar generator or bought in four di

shops.name: Magic box
shops.description: A magic box is a type of trap used in the Hunter skill. The magic box trapping technique is used to catch imps. With 71 Hunter, the player may set up a magic box. When an imp passes the trap, it may be caught, but only when the player is not standing on the trap.

shops.name: Noose wand
shops.description: The noose wand is used in the Hunter skill to catch kebbits while tracking. The kebbits caught through tracking are polar kebbits, common kebbits, feldip weasels, desert devils, and razor-backed kebbits.

shops.name: Bird snare
shops.description: A bird snare is a type of trap used in the Hunter skill. The bird snaring technique is used to catch several birds. With the required Hunter level, the player may set up a bird snare and wait for a bird to approach the trap.

shops.name: Box trap
shops.description: A box trap is a type of trap used in the Hunter skill to catch ferrets, embertailed jerboas and chinchompas. Using a box trap requires the specific Hunter level needed for the prey, as well as partial completion of the Eagles' Peak quest.

shops.name: Teasing stick
shops.description: A Teasing stick is used in Hunter to catch Spined larupia, Horned graahk, Sabre-toothed kyatt, sunlight antelope, and moonlight antelope. While carrying the teasing stick, players are able to "tease" these animals. This makes them aggressive toward the player, allowing players to lure them into a Pi

shops.name: Unlit torch
shops.description: An unlit torch may be used with a tinderbox or used on a fire or range to get a lit torch. It is useful for dark tunnels and smoking Hunter traps once lit. 39 Hunter is required to smoke traps. Smoking traps increases the chance of catching a creature by 2% (additive).

shops.name: Rabbit snare
shops.description: A rabbit snare is a type of trap used in the Hunter skill. The rabbit snaring technique is used to catch white rabbits. With 27 Hunter, completion of the Eagles' Peak quest, and a ferret in the inventory, the player may set up a rabbit snare and flush a rabbit hole to trap a white rabbit. When the r

shops.name: Bird snare pack
shops.description: Bird snare pack is an item pack containing 100 bird snares.

shops.name: Box trap pack
shops.description: Box trap pack is an item pack containing 100 box traps.

shops.name: Magic imp box pack
shops.description: Magic imp box pack is an item pack containing 100 magic boxes.

shops.name: Demonic Pacts League
shops.description: The Demonic Pacts League is the upcoming sixth League in Old School RuneScape. The name was revealed with a teaser trailer at the New Game+ Showcase 2026 on 8 January 2026, with further details coming as part of the Winter Summit 2026.

stock.Name: Endless Harvest
stock.Effect: Toggleable effect: All resources gathered will be sent to the bank. You can endlessly gather from fishing spots, trees, and mining rocks even if they deplete after your initial interaction. Resources gathered from Fishing, Woodcutting, and Mining are multiplied by 2. XP is granted for all additional resources gathered.

stock.Name: Barbarian Gathering
stock.Effect: Grants the knapsack Up to 3 different types of gathered items (wood, fish, metallic ore and coal) can be placed in the knapsack, which has a total maximum capacity of 140. Toggleable effect: dispose option will ask what to destroy The knapsack can be retrieved from the Leagues Tutor in Yama's lair if lost. You become capable of chopping wood, mining rocks and fishing with your bare hands without requiring any tools or bait. Your hands are equivalent to the crystal version of the respective tools where those exist. Whenever you gain Fishing, Woodcutting or Mining XP while gathering you will also receive an additional 10% XP in both Strength and Agility On failing to mine a rock, catch a fish or chop a tree you will have a separate 50% chance to succeed instead.

stock.Name: Abundance
stock.Effect: Toggleable effect: Coins generated will be put into your inventory. All non-combat skills are permanently boosted by 10. Every time you receive an XP drop, gain an additional 2 XP in the same skill. This is affected by the league passive XP modifier. For every XP gained, gain 2x as many coins. These can go either to your bank or inventory if there is room, which can be toggled above.

stock.Name: Hotfoot
stock.Effect: Upon choosing this relic you will receive the searing boots. While the searing boots are worn, you gain the following benefits: Agility experience is gained based on your Agility level while you run. Caught fish are automatically cooked. Mined ore is automatically smelted. Additionally, regardless of your footwear: Picking up some termites or a mark of grace from a course will automatically complete the lap granting completion XP and 10,000 coins. Marks of grace will always have a 33% chance to spawn on all rooftop courses. Completing a course grants two completion count and 25% bonus experience. 100% success rate on all actions for Agility and Cooking. You will receive 2x termites from the Colossal Wyrm Agility Course. You will receive 10x crystal shards from Prifddinas Agility Course. The searing boots can be retrieved from the Leagues tutor in Yama's lair if lost.

stock.Name: Friendly Forager
stock.Effect: Upon choosing this relic you will receive the forager's pouch. While the forager's pouch is worn or in your inventory, you gain the following benefits: When you gather resources from Woodcutting, Fishing, Mining, and Hunter, the pouch will find and store a random grimy herb This herb is limited to any which your Herblore level + 25 can clean. This action also provides a small amount of Herblore experience. The pouch will only find herbs it has room to store, but will always attempt to give you a herb you can receive. The pouch also functions as a silklined herb sack, and shares an inventory with it. Additionally, you gain the following perks to Herblore: All items are processed at once. Secondary ingredients have a 90% chance to not be consumed, this stacks additively with other sources. Potions created will have 4 doses instead of 3. The forager's pouch can be retrieved from the Leagues Tutor in Yama's Lair if lost.

stock.Name: Woodsman
stock.Effect: Toggleable effects: Hunter traps harvest directly to your bank. Logs chopped will be automatically burned. Passive effects: All items are processed at once while Fletching, stackable fletching items are capped at 10x the regular amount per action. Chopped logs are automatically burned while Woodcutting granting full Firemaking XP, this effect can be toggled above. 100% success rate on all Hunter actions. Hunter traps attract animals faster, give double the loot and XP. Hunter traps always drop a random herb seed or tree seed when harvested. Hunter rumours give double XP and Hunters loot sacks will award 2x as much loot. All loot from jarred implings will be doubled and noted. Additionally they will no longer break upon opening. All Quetzal Whistles will not lose charges.

stock.Name: Evil Eye
stock.Effect: Grants the evil eye The eye will allow you to teleport to the entrance of any boss or raid in the combat achievements list. Barrows will have the option to go to the chest or to the surface. You can teleport to each Moon of peril individually. The eye ignores wilderness teleport restrictions. The eye cannot be used to teleport to an area you haven't unlocked. The eye can be retrieved from the Leagues Tutor in Yama's Lair if lost.

stock.Name: Conniving Clues
stock.Effect: When opening a reward casket, you have a 1/3 chance to receive clue contracts which can be consumed to teleport you to your current clue step. Beginner clues give 0-2 contracts at a time. Easy clues give 1-4 contracts at a time. Medium clues give 1-5 contracts at a time. Hard clues give 1-7 contracts at a time. Elite clues give 1-9 contracts at a time. Master clues give 1-10 contracts at a time. Rewards caskets have a 1/4 chance to contain a clue scroll box of the same tier. Clues from creatures and impling jars now have a drop rate of 1/15. Clue vessels obtained from skilling (such as clue geodes or clue nests) are 10x more likely to drop. All clues have the lowest possible number of steps, and will give the maximum amount of reward rolls. Emote and Falo clue steps no longer have item requirements.

stock.Name: Nature's Accord
stock.Effect: Grants the fairy mushroom Active effects: Farming patches and plants no longer have any level requirements to harvest, plant, or make. Additionally, you gain the following perks to Farming: You will receive 10x yield from all farming patches and it will automatically be noted. XP is granted for all additional resources gathered. Your plants will never die. You have a 20% chance to not use a seed or sapling when planting crops in patches. The fairy mushroom will allow you to teleport to any fairy ring, spirit tree, or tool leprechaun. Unlocking this will autocomplete the Tree Gnome Village quest. This item ignores wilderness teleport restrictions. This item cannot be used to teleport to an area you haven't unlocked. The fairy mushroom can be retrieved from the Leagues tutor in Yama's Lair if lost.

stock.Name: Larcenist
stock.Effect: You will gain the following benefits while Thieving: 100% success rate on all actions. Automatically re-pickpocket an NPC. Automatically re-steal from a stall. Items obtained from pickpocketing and stalls are noted. Stalls never deplete and guards will never catch you thieving from them. The amount of coin pouches you can carry is increased by 10x Additionally, while Thieving, loot is increased by 10x for the following: Pickpocketing (not including clue scrolls) Stealing from stalls. Stealing house valuables in Varlamore.

stock.Name: Grimoire
stock.Effect: Grants the arcane grimoire The grimoire can be used to freely swap between spellbooks. The grimoire acts as a book of the dead. Additionally, when you select this relic, you will unlock access to all prayers and spells regardless of area, quest, or diary requirements. The arcane grimoire can be retrieved from the Leagues Tutor in Yama's Lair if lost.

stock.Name: Culling Spree
stock.Effect: Players may choose their slayer task from a list of 3 choices, with at least 1 boss task if possible Task kill count is selectable for all tasks (5-200) Superior slayer monsters have a 50% chance of spawning another superior on death Superior monsters always drop between 1 and 3 Clue scroll (elite) All effects of a Slayer helmet(i) are gained without needing to wear one All slayer reward shop perks are free The Rune pouch, Herb sack and Looting bag are purchasable from the slayer reward store.

stock.Name: Reloaded
stock.Effect: Choose another relic from any tier below this one.

stock.Name: Minion
stock.Effect: Grants the minion whistle The whistle will allow you to summon a powerful minion which lasts for 30 minutes. When you are in multi-combat it will cast an AoE attack if the target is weakest to range or magic. The minion's stats are: Min hit: 3 Max hit: 10 Attack speed: 1.8 Accuracy: 45,000 The whistle can consume up to 5 unique equippable Zamorak items by using them on the whistle, increasing your minion's max hit by 2 per unique item. The minion will try to automatically loot items from creatures you kill (including fired ranged ammo). A minimum value threshold and whether or not these should be noted is configurable on the whistle. The minion will deal damage to targets which are immune to thralls. The minion will not fight in PvP and will not fight against Yama. The minion whistle can be retrieved from the Leagues Tutor in Yama's Lair if lost.

stock.Name: Flask of Fervour
stock.Effect: Grants the flask of fervour When the Flask is consumed: Your Hitpoints will be restored to full. Your prayer points will be restored to full. Your special attack energy will be restored to full. Over the next 2.4 seconds the sigil will: Trigger three explosions which deal 60% of your base Prayer level (30% in PvP) to all enemies within 3 tiles of you as typeless damage. Reduce all damage you take to 0. Not deal damage to Yama. This item has a base cooldown of 3 minutes For every 10 damage you deal in a single hit, reduce the cooldown by 0.6s. The flask of fervour can be retrieved from the Leagues Tutor in Yama's Lair if lost.

shops.name: Demonic Pacts League/Areas/Kandarin
shops.description: The Kandarin area covers the entire Kingdom of Kandarin, along with the Feldip Hills, Ape Atoll and Crash Island. Instances of areas in Kandarin and underground areas with an entrance in Kandarin will also be included (with the exception of Taverley Dungeon and the Underground Pass).

stock.Source: Nuclear smoke devil and smoke devil

shops.name: Plant cure
shops.description: A plant cure potion is used on diseased plants, during Farming, to restore the plant to a healthy state so it continues to grow. Farming shops and GAG farmers at farming patches sell them for 40 coins each.

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Compost
shops.description: Compost is an item used in the Farming skill to reduce the chance of a farming patch becoming diseased per growth stage by 50 %. When used on a farming patch, each bucket grants 18 Farming experience.

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Rake
shops.description: Rakes are used in the Farming skill to remove weeds from farming patches. It can be bought from Farming shops. In addition, it can also be purchased from most of the gardeners who look after your farming patches. A tool leprechaun can store one for you.

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Empty plant pot
shops.description: Plant pots are used in the Farming skill to turn tree seeds into seedlings. With the appropriate Farming level and a gardening trowel in their inventory, a player can create a seedling by using a tree seed on a filled plant pot. After watering a seedling with a watering can, a few minutes later it w

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Watering can
shops.description: Watering cans are used in the Farming skill to water allotment, flower, and hops patches, as well as to water seedlings to grow saplings. Watering reduces the risk of disease occurring at the end of that growth cycle by varying amounts. During the next growth cycle, the plant moves from being watere

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Gardening trowel
shops.description: A gardening trowel is used in the members only skill Farming. It is used both to fill a plant pot with earth from a weeded patch, and to plant a tree seed into that plant pot to begin the process of growing a tree.

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Seed dibber
shops.description: A seed dibber is used to plant seeds in Farming patches. Up to 100 can be stored with tool leprechauns near Farming patches. They can be purchased from farming shops or any farmer that protects patches. An unlimited amount can be taken from a tool store 5.

stock.Location: Hops patch north of McGrubor's Wood

shops.name: Mac
shops.description: Mac is an NPC who is located west of the Warriors' Guild on a small island, beyond the ladder that leads into the guild's basement. Getting to this island requires the player to pass obstacles requiring 99 in their respective skills.

shops.name: Mac's island
shops.description: Mac's island is an island in Merwick Lake located west of the Warriors' Guild where Mac may be found. Here, players who have reached the maximum total level of 2376 are able to purchase Mac's cape for 2,376,000.

shops.name: Demonic Pacts League/Areas/Asgarnia
shops.description: The Asgarnia area covers the entire Kingdom of Asgarnia along with the island of Entrana, the Pest Control islands, and Troll Country. Instances of areas in Asgarnia and underground areas with an entrance in Asgarnia will also be included. Access to Catherby from White Wolf Mountain will be restrict

stock.Source: Kree'arra, General Graardor,Commander Zilyana, K'ril Tsutsaroth

shops.name: Varlamorian kebab
shops.description: Varlamorian kebabs can be purchased from Emelio after completion of the quest Meat and Greet, where the player helps him introduce kebabs to Varlamore. Like other kebabs, they heal a variable amount, varying between 3-30 hitpoints. Unlike other kebabs, they cannot drain your stats, and even have a c

shops.name: Battered book
shops.description: The battered book, titled Book of the Elemental Shield is a quest item used in Elemental Workshop I. It is found by searching the east bookcase in the building with a cooking range, south of the building with anvils in it in Seers' Village.

shops.name: Runes
shops.description: Runes are small, weightless, magical stones with symbols on them representing various elements. Players need the right combinations of runes to cast spells using the Magic skill.

stock.Runecraft: Used to cast air missiles and one of the four basic elemental runes.
Used to cast basic missile spells (Wind, Water, Earth and Fire Strike).
Used to cast water missiles and one of the four basic elemental runes.
Used to cast earth missiles and one of the four basic elemental runes.
Used to cast fire missiles and one of the four basic elemental runes.
Used to cast low level curse spells.
Used for item enchanting spells (jewellery, crossbow bolts, and orbs).
Used to cast low level missile spells (Wind, Water, Earth and Fire Bolt) as well as Crumble Undead.
Used for transmutation spells such as Low and High Level Alchemy and Superheat Item, as well as Binding.
Used to cast teleportation spells and Telekinetic Grab.
Used to cast medium level missile spells (Wind, Water, Earth and Fire Blast).

stock.Level: Stronger version of the fire rune, any successful hit with offensive fire spells will deal a minimum of 10% of the player's max hit.
Used to cast Lunar spells. Lunar Diplomacy is required to craft and use these runes.
Used to cast high level missile spells (Wind, Water, Earth and Fire Wave).
Used to cast high level curse spells, teleport-other spells and some more spells.
Used to cast very high level missile spells (Wind, Water, Earth and Fire Surge).

shops.name: Rune Essence mine
shops.description: The rune essence mine is a mine only accessible by being teleported there by various NPCs, and it is one of two locations where rune and pure essence can be mined; the other being the Lunar Isle Mine. It is only accessible after Rune Mysteries. Unlike the one located on Lunar Isle, this mine cannot

shops.name: Fur stall
shops.description: A fur stall is a stall that requires 35 Thieving to loot and grants 45 experience. Successfully looting a fur stall will give the player a piece of grey wolf fur. The thievable stalls are located in Ardougne, Rellekka, and Civitas illa Fortis.

shops.name: Estate Agent
shops.description: Estate agents are NPCs that sell players land to build player-owned houses (POH) on, as well as decorating them and/or moving them. These agents can be found in several major cities: Varrock, Falador, Seers' Village, East Ardougne, Hosidius, and Varlamore as well as Alwyn taking up the role in the I

shops.name: Player-owned house
shops.description: A player-owned house (often shortened to POH) is the focus of the Construction skill. It can be bought from an estate agent for 1,000, or received for free upon completion of the miniquest Daddy's Home. Players' houses are located in Rimmington by default but can be moved to one of 8 different citie

shops.name: Fancy Clothes Store
shops.description: The Fancy Clothes Store is a shop in south-eastern Varrock. It is owned and run by Asyff. The shop sells needles and thread and different kinds of clothes such as gloves, boots, capes and hats. The store is also one of the places where players can obtain hunter clothing, through the Custom Fur Cloth

stock.Furs required: Polar kebbit fur x2
Common kebbit fur x2
Feldip weasel fur x2
Desert devil fur x2
Larupia fur or Tatty larupia fur
Larupia fur or Tatty larupia fur
Graahk fur or Tatty graahk fur
Graahk fur or Tatty graahk fur
Kyatt fur or Tatty kyatt fur
Kyatt fur or Tatty kyatt fur

shops.name: Hunter
shops.description: Hunter is a members-only skill in which players catch different animals and creatures in RuneScape. Although Hunter is a non-combat skill, players can still be damaged while hunting. Using hunting equipment bought from Hunter stores, players can hunt specific creatures obtaining rewards such as spot

stock.Source: Sewn from a small pouch and 3 sunlight antelope furs
Sewn from a small pouch and 3 graahk furs
Sewn from a medium pouch and 3 kyatt furs

stock.What is stored: Small nets for net traps, diving equipment for drift net fishing and Molch pearls from aerial fishing

stock.Source: Slayer Rewards or Farmer Gricoller's Rewards from Tithe Farm

stock.What is stored: Logs for deadfall trapping and pitfall trapping

shops.name: Hunter armour
shops.description: Hunter gear is armour made with furs obtained from catching creatures with tracking or pitfalls using the Hunter skill. It cannot be crafted by players, instead requiring the use of the Custom Fur Clothing interface at the Fancy Clothes Store in Varrock, Pellem's Fur Store in the Hunter Guild, or Pr

stock.Name: Polar camouflage gear
stock.Notes: Each piece provides weight reduction when worn.

stock.Name: Wood camouflage gear
stock.Notes: Each piece provides weight reduction when worn.

stock.Name: Jungle camouflage gear
stock.Notes: Each piece provides weight reduction when worn.

stock.Name: Desert camouflage gear
stock.Notes: Each piece provides weight reduction when worn.

stock.Name: Larupia hunter gear
stock.Notes: The full set reduces damage taken from Hunter creatures by 20%.

stock.Name: Graahk hunter gear
stock.Notes: The full set reduces damage taken from Hunter creatures by 40%.

stock.Name: Kyatt hunter gear
stock.Notes: The full set reduces damage taken from Hunter creatures by 60%.

shops.name: Platebodies
shops.description: A platebody is a type of armour worn mainly by warriors. Among melee attack types, platebody provides good defence against the slash attack type but is more vulnerable to the crush attack type.

shops.name: Chainbodies
shops.description: A chainbody is a type of melee body armour. Its tendency to absorb impact makes it better for blocking crushing attacks. Rangers can utilise chainbodies to afford them some melee protection at the expense of magical defence; however, this will not raise their Ranged attack bonus as leather or dragon

shops.name: Platelegs
shops.description: Platelegs are a type of armour worn mainly by warriors in the leg slot. Among melee attack types, platelegs provides good defence against the slash attack type but is more vulnerable to the crush attack type. Platelegs gives a player penalties towards ranged attacks and penalties towards magic defen

shops.name: Leather armour
shops.description: Leather armour is the lowest quality ranged armour. There are no requirements to wear it. All of the pieces can be made using the Crafting skill from leather.

shops.name: Crossbow (weapon)
shops.description: Crossbows are a category of weapons used in ranged combat that require a certain Ranged level to wield. They are different than bows in that they are horizontal and can be held with one hand (except for the Karil's crossbow, Light ballista and Heavy ballista), allowing a player to carry a shield wit

shops.name: Ammunition
shops.description: Ammunition is worn equipment primarily in the ammunition slot that is usually consumed by Ranged weapons. Bows use arrows, crossbows use bolts, ballistae use javelins, and the toxic blowpipe uses darts. The level required on the tables below is equivalent to the weapon requirement to use that ammuni

shops.name: Quest Speedrunning
shops.description: Quest Speedrunning is a members-only game mode released on 12 October 2022. The game mode takes place on separate worlds and gives players a different save profile, similarly to Leagues. From there, players are able to choose a quest that they want to speedrun and will be given the relevant levels a

stock.Description: A cosmetic set of clothing representing Quest Speedrunning.
A cosmetic set of clothing representing Quest Speedrunning.
A cosmetic set of clothing representing Quest Speedrunning. Can be used on the graceful outfit to change its appearance.
A scroll that can be used to unlock a speedrunning themed Home Teleport animation.
A large, two-handed stopwatch that overrides the player's idle, walk, and run animations.

shops.name: Cape
shops.description: Cape is a term applied to any kind of equipable item worn in the cape slot that resembles a cape or cloak (as opposed to a backpack). Below are some of the many different types of cape that players can wear in Old School RuneScape.

shops.name: Gloves
shops.description: Gloves are pieces of armour that players can wear in the hands slot. These are the many different types of gloves that players can wear in Old School RuneScape.

stock.Name: Adamant gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing seven subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Antisanta gloves
stock.Notes: Reward for completing the 2014 Christmas event.

stock.Name: Barrows gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing the Recipe for Disaster quest.

stock.Name: Beekeeper's gloves
stock.Notes: Possible reward for completing the Beekeeper Random Event.

stock.Name: Black gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing five subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Bronze gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing two subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Cow gloves
stock.Notes: Reward for opening a birthday present during Old School RuneScape's birthdays.

stock.Name: Dragon gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing nine subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Ferocious gloves
stock.Notes: Created from Hydra leather after the Dragon Slayer II quest. Hydra leather is obtained as a drop from the Alchemical Hydra.

stock.Name: Ghostly gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained by completing a random section of the Curse of the Empty Lord miniquest.

stock.Name: Canifis gloves
stock.Notes: Obtainable after completing Priest in Peril from the Canifis Clothing store.

stock.Name: Fremennik gloves
stock.Notes: Bought from Yrsa's Clothing Shop in Rellekka.

stock.Name: Gloves of silence
stock.Notes: Made from dark kebbit fur. Slightly reduces chances of being caught while pickpocketing.

stock.Name: Graceful gloves
stock.Notes: Purchasable from Grace's shop within the Rogues' Den for 30 marks of grace.

stock.Name: Granite gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained as a drop from the Grotesque Guardians.

stock.Name: Ham gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained by pickpocketing H.A.M. members, or killing H.A.M. guards.

stock.Name: Hardleather gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing one subquest in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Ice gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained by killing the Ice Queen. Used in Heroes' Quest and Recipe for Disaster. Helpful in Desert Treasure I.

stock.Name: Infinity gloves
stock.Notes: Bought at the Mage Training Arena.

stock.Name: Iron gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing three subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Karamja gloves 1
stock.Notes: Reward for completing all the easy tasks in the Karamja Diary.

stock.Name: Karamja gloves 2
stock.Notes: Reward for completing all the medium tasks in the Karamja Diary.

stock.Name: Karamja gloves 3
stock.Notes: Reward for completing all the hard tasks in the Karamja Diary.

stock.Name: Karamja gloves 4
stock.Notes: Reward for completing all the elite tasks in the Karamja Diary.

stock.Name: Leather gloves
stock.Notes: Created using the Crafting skill from soft leather.

stock.Name: Mime gloves
stock.Notes: A possible reward from the Mime's random event.

stock.Name: Mithril gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing six subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Moonclan gloves
stock.Notes: Can be purchased from the clothing store on Lunar Isle after Lunar Diplomacy.

stock.Name: Mourner gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained during Mourning's End Part I.

stock.Name: Mystic gloves
stock.Notes: Blue mystic gloves can be bought in the Wizards' Guild while white and black mystic gloves can be obtained as monster drops from certain Slayer monsters.

stock.Name: Penance gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained by exchanging Honour Points in the Barbarian Assault minigame. One of the few items that reduce weight.

stock.Name: Rockshell gloves
stock.Notes: Dropped by Giant rock crabs at Waterbirth Island.

stock.Name: Rogue gloves
stock.Notes: Possible reward for completing the Rogues' Den minigame.

stock.Name: Rune gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing eight subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Santa gloves
stock.Notes: Reward for completing the 2014 Christmas event.

stock.Name: Skeletal gloves
stock.Notes: Obtained as a drop from Wallasalki.

stock.Name: Slayer gloves
stock.Notes: Bought from any Slayer Master. Worn to fight Fever spiders.

stock.Name: Spined gloves
stock.Notes: Dropped by Dagannoths at Waterbirth Island.

stock.Name: Steel gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing four subquests in Recipe for Disaster.

stock.Name: Void knight gloves
stock.Notes: Reward from the Pest Control minigame.

stock.Name: White gloves
stock.Notes: Special metal gloves that can be purchased after completing Wanted!.

stock.Name: Zombie gloves
stock.Notes: A possible reward from Leo's random event.

stock.Name: Mining gloves
stock.Notes: a pair of gloves purchasable from Belona's shop inside the Mining Guild.

stock.Name: Superior mining gloves
stock.Notes: a pair of gloves purchasable from Belona's shop inside the Mining Guild.

stock.Name: Expert mining gloves
stock.Notes: a pair of gloves purchasable from Belona's shop inside the Mining Guild.

shops.name: Boots
shops.description: Boots are armour equipable in the feet slot. Below are some of the many different boots that players can wear in Old School RuneScape.

shops.name: Scythe (Halloween event)
shops.description: The scythe is an untradeable event reward. It can be obtained by completing one of the Halloween holiday events that occur once per year.

shops.name: Bunny ears
shops.description: Bunny ears are an untradeable cosmetic holiday item worn on the head. A pair of bunny ears can be obtained by completing one of the Easter holiday events that occur once per year. This also rewards the player with all other holiday items associated with Easter.

shops.name: Frog token
shops.description: A Frog token is a reward from the Kiss the frog random event. These tokens can be given to Thessalia in Varrock in exchange for parts of the royal frog costume, a frog mask, or a lamp.

shops.name: Frog
shops.description: Frogs can be found in the Lumbridge Swamp. They are the smallest and weakest type of frog. They were released with the permanent free-to-play update to help provide new combat training options for the large amounts of new players.

shops.name: Frog mask
shops.description: The Frog mask can only be obtained from the Kiss the frog random event, which rewards you with a Frog token. Players can then proceed to the Thessalia's Fine Clothes store in Varrock to redeem the token in exchange for the Frog mask, parts of the royal frog costume or a Lamp.

shops.name: Royal frog costume
shops.description: The royal frog costume is a set of clothing awarded from the Kiss the frog random event. The outfit cannot be traded to other players or sold anywhere.

shops.name: Lamp
shops.description: The lamp is a reward obtained from several random events or by opening an Intricate Pouch that differs from those awarded by quests. While these lamps cannot be traded or banked, rubbing one gives the option of adding experience to any skill a player chooses. The amount given is ten times their skil

shops.name: Apothecary
shops.description: Ingald Belger the Apothecary runs the pharmacy and potion shop in south-west Varrock. He plays a part in certain quests, often making delicate potions and herbal products.

shops.name: Strength potions
shops.description: A Strength potion is a potion that can be made by free-to-play players in addition to members. Both non-members and members can make a strength potion (4) by providing the Apothecary in Varrock with 5 coins, a limpwurt root, and red spiders' eggs. Members who have attained at least level 12 Herblore

stock.Location: ground floor1st floor of the Warriors' Guild

shops.name: Energy potions
shops.description: An Energy potion restores 10% of the player's run energy.

shops.name: Newcomer map
shops.description: A Newcomer map is a map sold at many general stores for one coin. It is also sold by Rasolo and Trader crewmembers for 2 coins.

shops.name: Varrock Herald
shops.description: A newspaper is a readable item that tells about the different going-ons in and around Varrock at that time. Players can purchase an issue for 50 coins from Benny in Varrock Square. Benny's shouts reveal that it is called the Varrock Herald. Buying a newspaper is required for the Easy Varrock Diary.

shops.name: Bronze dagger
shops.description: The bronze dagger is the most basic dagger in Old School RuneScape, and can be made at an anvil using the Smithing skill. This requires one bronze bar and a hammer, and yields 12.5 Smithing experience. There are no level requirements to create or wield a bronze dagger; like many melee weapons, it ca

stock.STASH location: Outside the shack that leads to Zanaris in Lumbridge Swamp

shops.name: Iron dagger
shops.description: The iron dagger is a dagger stronger than the bronze dagger. Players can make an iron dagger with the Smithing skill at level 15 using 1 iron bar, giving the player 25 Smithing experience. Members can poison this weapon, giving them an advantage in combat. Players can purchase this dagger from the V

shops.name: Steel dagger
shops.description: The steel dagger is the third-weakest dagger, requiring level 5 Attack to wield. They can be bought from shops and obtained drops from monsters, and can also be smithed from a steel bar at level 30 Smithing. As with most daggers, steel daggers can be poisoned.

shops.name: Black dagger
shops.description: The black dagger is a dagger slightly more accurate than the steel dagger. Like most daggers it can be poisoned, giving an advantage in combat. Like all black weapons, wielding it requires an Attack level of 10.

shops.name: Mithril dagger
shops.description: The mithril dagger is a dagger made out of mithril. Mithril daggers can be smithed from a single mithril bar at level 50 Smithing. Each mithril dagger created grants 50 experience. Mithril daggers are the fifth strongest daggers, the next strongest being the adamant dagger. Like all mithril weapons,

shops.name: Adamant dagger
shops.description: The adamant dagger is a dagger made of adamantite. It can be created by using one Adamant bar and a hammer on an anvil at level 70 Smithing, granting 62.5 Smithing experience. As with most daggers, it can be poisoned.

stock.STASH location: Ground floor of the Arceuus Library centre east

shops.name: Dagger
shops.description: Daggers are generally small, fast melee weapons. Most daggers are stab swords which primarily use stab attack styles but also have an option for a slashing style; though there are many items thought of as daggers that either belong to different weapon categories or aren't even wieldable.

shops.name: Sword
shops.description: Swords, also referred to by players as shortswords, are fast stabbing weapons that are stronger than daggers. While a sword is best at stabbing, players can also use it as a slashing weapon. It is generally a good weapon with all-round stats and isn't too expensive. Like most melee weapons, swords c

shops.name: Longsword
shops.description: Longswords are medium-speed slashing weapons used in melee combat. While they have a slower attack speed than scimitars, longswords are able to hit higher in a single attack. Many players prefer them to two-handed swords both because a shield can be equipped with a longsword, and because longswords

shops.name: Zaff's Superior Staffs
shops.description: Zaff's Superior Staffs is a staff shop located near central Varrock, owned by Zaff. It is the main source of battlestaves and of elemental staves for free players.

shops.name: Magic
shops.description: Magic is a skill which allows players to cast different kinds of spells for various purposes, and forms one of the three combat classes in Old School RuneScape. There are broadly three categories of spells: combat spells used in combat, teleportation spells enabling efficient travel across the world

shops.name: Battlestaves
shops.description: Battlestaves are combined melee and magical weapons which can be used in combat. They are primary used for casting combat spells, having +2 more Magic Attack bonuses compared to standard staves, although they also have Melee Attack bonuses.

stock.Name: ^ Untradeable; price is of the tormented synapse, iron bar, and battlestaff used to create the staff. ^ Reverts to standard Slayer's staff when it runs out of charges, and needs to have a Slayer's enchantment reapplied. ^ 3.0 3.1 Is not shown on Equipment Stats, and applies only to the standard spellbook. ^ Cost to repair the broken staff. ^ Cost to repair the broken staff and upgrade it to Iban's staff (u), which holds 2,500 charges instead of 120. ^ Costs 250 Void Knight commendation points.

shops.name: Crafting
shops.description: Crafting is a skill that allows players to create items such as jewellery, pottery, and armour for use or for trade.

shops.name: High Alchemy
shops.description: High Level Alchemy (also known as high alch, hi alch, or HA) is a utility spell available in the standard spellbook. It requires level 55 Magic to cast, and grants 65 Magic experience per cast.

stock.Name: leagues4_highlvl_alchemy_01
stock.Description: Casting the spell with the Leagues IV - Trailblazer reloaded animation override
